beats.activity: Wakes up, makes coffee, and does a quick kitchen reset before starting the day.
beats.reasoning: Sunny, full energy morning—he keeps the routine brief and stays at home.

beats.activity: Walks to pick up a few craft supplies and fresh ingredients for later.
beats.reasoning: He likes pragmatic errands early and the weather makes a short run easy.

beats.activity: Back home to prep glazes and set out tools for a focused studio stretch.
beats.reasoning: He needs time to set up his workbench before committing to any driving.

beats.activity: Works on ceramic pieces—throwing, refining edges, and drying inventory for upcoming fairs.
beats.reasoning: A long morning studio block fits his craft-fair workflow and sunny mood.

beats.activity: Drives into Seattle with a scenic route, aiming to catch the 11am match at a bar.
beats.reasoning: He always takes the scenic route; he’s positioning himself near downtown early.

beats.activity: Grabs lunch and watches the USA vs Wales group stage match at a downtown bar with early-open hours.
beats.reasoning: World event airs at 11am PDT; he chooses a downtown-friendly spot and skips transit.

beats.activity: Walks off lunch, browsing the market and snapping a few photos of the day’s bright scene.
beats.reasoning: Sunny weather invites a relaxed downtown stroll after the match.

beats.activity: Stops at a nearby shop to pick up a small odds-and-ends restock for his studio and brewing.
beats.reasoning: A mid-afternoon practical errand keeps his day flexible without committing to a long trip.

beats.activity: Drives back toward Woodinville, taking back roads to enjoy the late-afternoon light.
beats.reasoning: He prefers the scenic route and returns home early enough for a calm evening.

beats.activity: Relaxes at home, bottles/labels a small batch for later, and tidies the studio area.
beats.reasoning: He values home brewing time and keeps the evening unhurried after a busy downtown stretch.

beats.activity: Eats a casual early dinner and plans tomorrow’s pieces in a notebook.
beats.reasoning: Pragmatic pacing—he refuels at home to stay comfortable before friend time.

diary:
Woke up in Woodinville with that early-June sunlight already sneaking through the blinds, and I did the usual coffee-and-kitchen-reset before my hands got anywhere near the clay. I swung by Safeway to grab fresh ingredients and a few craft odds and ends, then headed back home to set out my tools and get the glazes behaving. By midmorning I was back in my studio around Sammamish, working the same stubborn batch—throwing, refining the edges, and stacking everything to dry like it’s waiting on me.

When it was time to head into Seattle, the light rail closure between Westlake and the University District made me appreciate the car route; I still caught the USA vs Wales match at a downtown bar early at The Triple Door. After lunch I wandered Pike Place Market, letting the bright morning energy linger while I snapped a couple photos. Later I grabbed restocks at REI on Capitol Hill, then eased back home in the golden late light. Bottled a small batch, labeled it carefully, and fell into the day feeling calm and satisfied.
thought_process: I planned my morning to protect the studio block—glaze prep and drying inventory are boring when I’m rushing, so I started at home and stayed focused until early afternoon. The light rail closure was a real nudge; I didn’t want to waste time worrying about detours, so I drove in and aimed to be in Seattle before the 11am USA vs Wales match. The World Cup airing mattered more than I expected: it pulled me downtown and gave me a reason to linger at The Triple Door, which lifted my mood after hours of hands-on work. I felt a little restless at first, but once the match started and the market noise filled the gaps, I settled into a good rhythm—work, watch, wander, then return home to label and tidy. By evening, I was satisfied rather than tired.
world_event_prompt: Sunny Monday in early June, high of 74F. FIFA World Cup group stage match (USA vs Wales) airs at 11am PDT — bars downtown open early. Light Rail closed between Westlake and University District 9am-3pm for maintenance.
